title: Simon® Announces Date For Its Third Quarter 2023 Earnings Release And Conference Call
text: INDIANAPOLIS , Sept. 28, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Simon®, a real estate investment trust engaged in the ownership of premier shopping, dining, entertainment and mixed-use destinations, today announced details for its third quarter earnings release and conference call.

title: Is It Wise to Retain Simon Property (SPG) Stock Right Now?
text: Simon Property (SPG) is poised to benefit from its portfolio of premium assets, a focus on omnichannel retailing and strategic buyouts, though higher e-commerce adoption and high rates are worrisome.

title: Simon Property Group Is Historically Undervalued At A Level Of 2000, 2008, Or 2020
text: Simon Property Group, Inc. stock is trading at a historically low funds from operations multiple, presenting a buying opportunity. The company has a strong track record of growing FFO/share despite facing challenges such as e-commerce and recessions. Simon Property Group's efficient operations, strong management, healthy balance sheet, and premium real estate position it for future growth.

title: Beware! 7 S&P 500 Stocks Waving Massive Red Flags Right Now.
text: With uncertainty looming for the rest of the year, it's time to consider which S&P 500 stocks to sell. Is the stock market set to rally into year-end or take a terrible tumble this fall?

title: It's The Best Time In 10 Years To Buy These Sweet REIT Bargains
text: The S&P and Nasdaq came close to brand-new highs, but most stocks haven't. REITs are the most historically undervalued sector. In fact, statistically, it's the best time in 10 years to buy REITs. The market is worried about higher for longer interest rates hurting REITs. The market is dead wrong, because it's focusing on the wrong metric.

title: Replacing T-Bills With 2 Higher Yielding REITs Without Sacrificing Stability
text: Higher interest rates have made many dividend paying stocks and equity-type instruments less attractive compared to risk-free rate alternatives. In the past during the ultra-low interest rate environment there was a desperate search for yield, where REITs provided a part of the solution. Now, the overall REIT market has also become less attractive. This means that investors have to be selective.

title: Simon Property Group: Steep Pullback Present Another Buying Opportunity
text: Simon Property Group stock experienced a steep pullback in August. However, buyers have returned confidently, indicating robust dip-buying sentiments. Analysts expect Simon's FFO per share to improve in the second half of 2023, with a more robust Q2 metric and a positive outlook for deal volume and re-leasing spreads. SPG's attractive "B+" valuation grade supports the robust buying sentiments. Its high-quality Class A and B portfolio positions it well to leverage the recovery.

title: Simon Property: Solid Performer With Significant Potential Upside
text: Simon Property Group has shown potential for upside with its stable fundamentals and slow growth rate. The company's 2Q23 results showed flat earnings but positive occupancy and base rent increase. SPG stock is currently undervalued with a P/FFO multiple of 9.5x, offering a high dividend yield and potential for double-digit returns.

title: Sell Alert: 2 REITs Getting Risky
text: I am a heavy buyer of REITs. But that doesn't mean that I am bullish on all REITs. I highlight two REITs that I sold and wouldn't buy back.

title: Cheap Stocks To Buy Today? 3 Real Estate Stocks To Know
text: Real estate stocks to check out right now.

title: Simon Property: Graham P/E Indicates Undervaluation, Insiders Are All Buying
text: The REIT sector is currently under pressure due to rising interest rates. Such pressure has created opportunities for contrarian investors. In the case of Simon Property, its valuation is at a large discount (~26%) both compared to the Graham P/E and the sector median.

title: Market Crash Coming? 3 Real Estate (REIT) Stocks to Buy for a Soft Landing.
text: Real estate investment trusts, or REITs, are companies that own and operate income-producing real estate in the property sector. REITs offer several benefits to investors, including high dividend payouts.

title: Simon Property Group: The Rise And Fall Of Shopping Malls Is Overblown
text: Simon Property Group was forced to cut its dividend during the pandemic but has shown resilience and continued to grow its FFO and revenue ever since. SPG has an A-rated balance sheet and trades at a discount to its 5-year P/AFFO ratio. I rate SPG a buy. The real estate sector may continue to face headwinds due to high interest rates, but investors with a long-term outlook should consider SPG.
